heading: Advances to Sergeant at Arms of Senate for extraordinary expenses
label: 6613 Advances to Sergeant at Arms of Senate for extraordinary expenses
text: The Secretary of the Senate on and after , is authorized, in his discretion, to advance to the Sergeant at Arms of the Senate such sums as may be necessary, not exceeding $4,000, to meet any extraordinary expenses of the Senate.July 31, 1958
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 65b of this title
Amendments1977— struck out “during any fiscal year” after “$4,000”.Pub. L. 95–261976— substituted “$4,000 during any fiscal year” for “$2,000”.Pub. L. 94–440
amendments: Amendments1977— struck out “during any fiscal year” after “$4,000”.Pub. L. 95–261976— substituted “$4,000 during any fiscal year” for “$2,000”.Pub. L. 94–440
